top_10.rationale: SimCLR: 'A Simple Framework for Contrastive Learning of Visual Representations' (Chen et al., 7321 citations) — the seminal and most-cited paper defining the modern contrastive self-supervised learning paradigm.
'A Survey on Contrastive Self-Supervised Learning' (1509 citations) — comprehensive review of pretext tasks, architectures, and performance comparisons across vision, NLP, and other domains.
Lilian Weng's 'Self-Supervised Representation Learning' blog — authoritative survey covering generative and contrastive SSL methods, widely referenced practitioner and researcher reference.
'Supervised Contrastive Learning' (Khosla et al.) — extends self-supervised contrastive learning to the supervised setting, foundational paper for understanding the broader contrastive framework.
'A Survey on Contrastive Self-supervised Learning' — systematic survey of self-defined pseudo-label methods and downstream task transfer, provides architectural taxonomy.
Comprehensive survey on contrastive learning — recent 2024 survey covering main ideas, developments, and applications in CV and NLP.
Ankesh Anand's blog on contrastive self-supervised learning — clear explanation of the principle (learning by encoding what makes two things similar/different), accessible entry point.

Theoretical understanding of self-supervised contrastive learning — introduces prototype representation bias and balanced contrastive loss, explains the theoretical underpinning of established practices.
GraphCL: Contrastive Self-Supervised Learning of Graph Representations — extends the framework to graph data, demonstrating the approach's generality beyond vision.
Supervised Contrastive Learning (NeurIPS 2020 proceedings version) — conference-published version of the supervised extension to contrastive learning.
